[OVERVIEW_ Homogeneous LoRA Rank in Federated Learning for Image Classification .md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26167189/OVERVIEW_.Homogeneous.LoRA.Rank.in.Federated.Learning.for.Image.Classification.md)
**Investigating the Impact of Homogeneous LoRA Rank in Federated Learning for Image Classification**  
**Project 1, Overview**  
---

![][image1]

Gabriel Jason William

21 March 2026

# **ABSTRACT**

This study investigates the effect of homogeneous Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) configurations in a federated learning environment. LoRA is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that reduces the number of trainable parameters by introducing low-rank updates. In this work, we evaluate how different fixed LoRA ranks influence model performance when applied uniformly across all clients in a federated setting.

Using a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on the [CIFAR-10 dataset](https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~kriz/cifar.html), we conduct experiments with varying LoRA ranks. The results demonstrate the relationship between rank size, model accuracy, and convergence behavior. 

# **CHAPTER I:**

# **INTRODUCTION**

**1.1. Objectives**

This research focuses on a simplified yet important scenario: **homogeneous LoRA rank**, where all clients use the same rank value. This research also provides a foundational baseline for understanding LoRA in federated learning. The findings will support future work on:

* Adaptive rank adjustment  
* Heterogeneous LoRA configurations  
* Decentralized federated learning systems

**1.2. Dataset**

This study uses the CIFAR-10 dataset, a standard benchmark dataset for image classification.

* Total images: 60,000  
* Training set: 50,000  
* Test set: 10,000  
* Image size: 32 × 32 RGB  
* Number of classes: 10

The dataset includes categories such as airplanes, automobiles, birds, cats, and trucks.

**To simulate a federated learning environment:**

* The training dataset is divided into multiple subsets, each representing a **client**  
* Each client trains the model using only its local data  
* The global model is evaluated using the centralized test dataset

This setup reflects real-world distributed learning scenarios where data is decentralized and cannot be shared directly.

# **CHAPTER II:**

# **Methodology**

**2.1. Model Architecture.**

A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is used for image classification. LoRA is applied to the fully connected layers to reduce the number of trainable parameters.  All clients use the same LoRA rank (r). Multiple experiments are conducted using different rank values (for example, r \= 2, 4, 8).

**2.2. Federated Learning Process.**

The training follows a standard Federated Averaging (FedAvg) procedure:

* A global model is initialized  
* Each client receives the global model  
* Clients train locally on their own data  
* Model updates are sent back and averaged  
* The global model is updated

This process is repeated for several communication rounds.

**2.3. Expected Output.**

It is expected that:

* Lower ranks will result in faster but less accurate models  
* Higher ranks will improve accuracy but increase computational cost  
* An optimal rank exists that balances efficiency and performance
